summary:
Portable ultrasonography has been shown to guide instrument insertion but lacks sufficient structural resolution to accurately determine Schanz pin location or depth within bone after placement. Radiographs remain the gold standard for verifying proper pin positioning due to superior image quality and ability to provide precise anatomic localization; there are no validated studies supporting ultrasound’s use for final evaluation of pin placement. 

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{No}}$

The provided information indicates that although ultrasound can assist in placing pins by providing real-time feedback during insertion, it does not offer adequate diagnostic precision—especially regarding exact depth and skeletal integration—to reliably confirm successful placement once surgery concludes. Without comparison data showing equivalent outcomes when matched against conventional x-rays, we conclude that ultrasound cannot currently replace radiologic methods in assessing fixed implants like Schanz pins.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{No}}$
